name: Sigmund Romberg
abstract: Sigmund Romberg (July 29, 1887 – November 9, 1951) was a Hungarian-born American composer. He is best known for his musicals and operettas, particularly The Student Prince (1924), The Desert Song (1926) and The New Moon (1928).

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: I decided we would play the best music written by the best American composers, which ran anywhere from Sigmund Romberg on up through Jerome Kern, Cole Porter, and whatever.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: Sigmund Romberg, Victor Herbert... that’s what changed jazz. That’s what opened the jazz musicians’ ears.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001
